patch.value:
## Context

`src/core/search/corpus.ts:115-138`:
```ts
const corpus: Record<string, unknown> = {
  title, description, tags, status, body,
  comments, notes, learnings, reminders, events, dependencies
};
```

Fields absent: `type`, `priority`, `assignee`, `parent`, `goal`, `value`, `why_now`, `risk`, `confidence`, `estimate`, `ac` (acceptance criteria), `resolution`, `expected`, `actual`.

For keyword search the front-matter cache covers type/status/priority as filter dimensions, but semantic search embeds only the corpus JSON string. A query like 'high priority bugs blocking release' cannot semantically match on `priority: 0` because that field is not in the corpus.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Add `type`, `priority`, `assignee`, `goal`, `value`, `why_now`, `ac`, `risk`, `confidence` to the semantic corpus
- [ ] Make the corpus field inclusion config-driven so teams can opt fields in/out
- [ ] Add `estimate` and `resolution` for richer closed-item recall
- [ ] Evaluate corpus character budget impact and adjust `OLLAMA_SEMANTIC_CORPUS_INPUT_MAX_CHARACTERS` or truncation policy if needed

## Evidence

- `src/core/search/corpus.ts:115-138` — corpus fields list; no type/priority/assignee
- `src/types.ts` — `ItemMetadata` has goal, value, why_now, ac, risk, confidence, estimate, assignee, parent
Search corpus: include type, priority, parent, assignee fields in keyword and semantic corpus

The search corpus builder (buildSearchCorpus) includes title, description, tags, status, body, comments, notes, learnings, reminders, events, dependencies, and plan fields. It does NOT include type, priority, parent, assignee, goal, or value fields. Searching for 'epic high priority' or 'assigned to alice' via semantic search cannot match on these dimensions because they are absent from the embedded corpus.

- Add `type`, `priority`, `assignee`, `goal`, `value`, `why_now`, `ac`, `risk`, `confidence` to the semantic corpus
- Make the corpus field inclusion config-driven so teams can opt fields in/out
- Add `estimate` and `resolution` for richer closed-item recall
- Evaluate corpus character budget impact and adjust `OLLAMA_SEMANTIC_CORPUS_INPUT_MAX_CHARACTERS` or truncation policy if needed

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Scoping (2026-06-14): buildSearchCorpus (core/search/corpus.ts) omits type/priority/assignee/parent/goal/value/why_now/ac/risk/confidence. Add them + config-driven inclusion (search.corpus_fields). Bundled into PR feat/search-relevance-filter-parity-corpus.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Enriched semantic search corpus + made field inclusion config-driven. Files: src/core/search/corpus.ts (logic), src/types.ts (PmSettings.search.corpus_fields?: string[]), src/core/store/settings-validator.ts (corpus_fields: vOptional(vArray(vString))), docs/CONFIGURATION.md, NEW tests/unit/core/search/corpus-fields.spec.ts. (a) Exact ItemMetadata field names added to corpus: type, priority, assignee, parent, goal, value, why_now, risk, confidence, estimated_minutes, acceptance_criteria, resolution, expected_result, actual_result (NOTE: real fields are expected_result/actual_result NOT expected/actual). Optional structured fields are omitted when absent/empty for token efficiency; the original always-on fields (title/description/tags/status/body/comments/notes/learnings/reminders/events/dependencies/plan) keep prior behavior. (b) Config key design: search.corpus_fields optional string[]; unset/empty/non-array => full DEFAULT_SEARCH_CORPUS_FIELDS (backward compatible), set => only those names (unknowns ignored). Added exported DEFAULT_SEARCH_CORPUS_FIELDS const + resolveSearchCorpusFields(settings) resolver + buildSearchCorpus/buildSemanticCorpusInput now accept {fields?}. NOT a nested-settings scalar (array kind unsupported; nested-settings only string/boolean/integer/number/ratio) -> documented hand-edit of settings.json instead. (c) Staleness: changing corpus_fields (or shipping new embedded fields) changes embedding input -> items flagged stale + re-embedded on next refresh/reindex. Expected and self-healing. (d) Tests: tests/unit/core/search/corpus-fields.spec.ts (19 pass w/ existing spec). Run: npx vitest run tests/unit/core/search/corpus-fields.spec.ts tests/unit/search-corpus.spec.ts

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Cross-cutting (lead): threaded resolveSearchCorpusFields(settings) into the 3 caller sites the corpus agent flagged — reindex.ts buildKeywordRecord (optional corpusFields param + keyword artifact map), reindex.ts executeReindexEmbedding semantic corpus inputs, and cache.ts embedLocatedSemanticVectors. search.corpus_fields config key is now LIVE end-to-end. Build + typecheck clean.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Shipped: buildSearchCorpus enriched with type/priority/assignee/parent/goal/value/why_now/risk/confidence/estimated_minutes/acceptance_criteria/resolution/expected_result/actual_result (omitted when empty for token efficiency). Config-driven via search.corpus_fields (unset=full default set; subset opt-in). resolveSearchCorpusFields threaded into reindex keyword+semantic artifacts and cache semantic refresh. Adding fields invalidates existing embeddings -> self-healing re-embed on next refresh. 19 new tests + validator + types + CONFIGURATION.md.
